name: ursocket

metadata.name: URsocket
metadata.summary: This package is built and tested for universal robot with polyscope version 5.12

documentation:
This package is tested on 5.12 polyscope version. 
It can be used from various environments. The only thing that it needs is a python>=3.9
Documentation will be uploaded soon here: "https://github.com/SarojDebnath/URsocket"
Also, Contribution to this package is welcomed highly.

documentation: ascii v

class_variables.name: URsocket.rtde.Command.RTDE_TEXT_MESSAGE
documentation: ascii M

documentation: ascii U

class_variables.name: URsocket.rtde.Command.RTDE_CONTROL_PACKAGE_SETUP_OUTPUTS
documentation: ascii O

class_variables.name: URsocket.rtde.Command.RTDE_CONTROL_PACKAGE_SETUP_INPUTS

methods.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper.__init__
documentation: Constructor.

methods.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper.connect
documentation:
Connects to a gripper at the given address.
\:param hostname: Hostname or ip.
\:param port: Port.
\:param socket_timeout: Timeout for blocking socket operations.

methods.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper.disconnect
documentation: Closes the connection with the gripper.

methods.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper._set_vars
documentation:
Sends the appropriate command via socket to set the value of n variables, and waits for its 'ack' response.
\:param var_dict: Dictionary of variables to set (variable_name, value).
\:return: True on successful reception of ack, false if no ack was received, indicating the set may not
have been effective.

methods.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper._set_var
documentation:
Sends the appropriate command via socket to set the value of a variable, and waits for its 'ack' response.
\:param variable: Variable to set.
\:param value: Value to set for the variable.
\:return: True on successful reception of ack, false if no ack was received, indicating the set may not
have been effective.

methods.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper._get_var
documentation:
Sends the appropriate command to retrieve the value of a variable from the gripper, blocking until the
response is received or the socket times out.
\:param variable: Name of the variable to retrieve.
\:return: Value of the variable as integer.

methods.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper._reset
documentation:
Reset the gripper.
The following code is executed in the corresponding script function
def rq_reset(gripper_socket="1"):
    rq_set_var("ACT", 0, gripper_socket)
    rq_set_var("ATR", 0, gripper_socket)

    while(not rq_get_var("ACT", 1, gripper_socket) == 0 or not rq_get_var("STA", 1, gripper_socket) == 0):
        rq_set_var("ACT", 0, gripper_socket)
        rq_set_var("ATR", 0, gripper_socket)
        sync()
    end

    sleep(0.5)
end

methods.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper.move
documentation:
Sends commands to start moving towards the given position, with the specified speed and force.
\:param position: Position to move to [min_position, max_position]
\:param speed: Speed to move at [min_speed, max_speed]
\:param force: Force to use [min_force, max_force]
\:return: A tuple with a bool indicating whether the action it was successfully sent, and an integer with
the actual position that was requested, after being adjusted to the min/max calibrated range.

methods.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper.move_and_wait_for_pos
documentation:
Sends commands to start moving towards the given position, with the specified speed and force, and
then waits for the move to complete.
\:param position: Position to move to [min_position, max_position]
\:param speed: Speed to move at [min_speed, max_speed]
\:param force: Force to use [min_force, max_force]
\:return: A tuple with an integer representing the last position returned by the gripper after it notified
that the move had completed, a status indicating how the move ended (see ObjectStatus enum for details). Note
that it is possible that the position was not reached, if an object was detected during motion.

class_variables.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper.ACT
documentation: act : activate (1 while activated, can be reset to clear fault status)

class_variables.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper.GTO
documentation: gto : go to (will perform go to with the actions set in pos, for, spe)

class_variables.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper.ATR
documentation: atr : auto-release (emergency slow move)

class_variables.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper.ADR
documentation: adr : auto-release direction (open(1) or close(0) during auto-release)

class_variables.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper.FOR
documentation: for : force (0-255)

class_variables.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper.SPE
documentation: spe : speed (0-255)

class_variables.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper.POS
documentation: pos : position (0-255), 0 = open

class_variables.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper.STA
documentation: status (0 = is reset, 1 = activating, 3 = active)

class_variables.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper.PRE
documentation: position request (echo of last commanded position)

class_variables.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper.OBJ
documentation: object detection (0 = moving, 1 = outer grip, 2 = inner grip, 3 = no object at rest)

class_variables.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper.FLT
documentation: fault (0=ok, see manual for errors if not zero)

class_variables.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper.PCO
documentation: 0= no connection problem

class_variables.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper.ENCODING
documentation: ASCII and UTF-8 both seem to work

inner_classes.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper.GripperStatus
documentation: Gripper status reported by the gripper. The integer values have to match what the gripper sends.

inner_classes.name: URsocket.robotiq.RobotiqGripper.ObjectStatus
documentation: Object status reported by the gripper. The integer values have to match what the gripper sends.
